text:
There once was a very lonely old owl. You see, he couldn't fly very well these days. So he spent his mornings reading books from his library. One book he loved had stories about brave little birds.

One dusky evening, feeling quite excited, he read aloud. As the tale ended, he felt truly inspired. He decided he'd become nocturnal; he'd sleep during the day and fly around at night. He had new friends at the bird sanctuary they visited once.

The owl flew westward, learning about new kinds of trees and flowers. He met squirrels, raccoons, and even bats. Life was not lonely anymore.

He returned to his ancient tree, feeling proud. His nocturnal life had grown interesting. But he missed his books.

So he returned to them in the light. Every evening he'd read one of those brave bird stories. By morning, he felt content.

This owl had found a new kind of love—one for stories. And he'd
